meta.note: Faithful, extractive summaries; every item links to its original source.

items.headline: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
items.summary: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.

items.headline: A taxonomy of omnicidal futures involving artificial intelligence (2025)
items.summary: 61 points and 48 comments on Hacker News.

items.headline: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
items.summary: Amazon is launching an update to its Alexa Plus assistant that will allow it to connect to smart home devices in new ways. With the update, which is currently in preview, Alexa Plus can link up with tech from Bosch, Delta, Ecovacs, iRobot,…

items.headline: AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system
items.summary: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

items.headline: AI Kill Switch Act would let Trump admin order shutdown of rogue AI systems
items.summary: Bill would let Homeland Security chief decide when an AI should be shut down.

items.headline: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
items.summary: Until now, voice mode has only been available on Claude Haiku, Anthropic's faster but less powerful model. Now the company is making its Opus and Sonnet models available in voice mode, and extending its reach into apps like Gmail, Slack,…

items.headline: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
items.summary: Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email.

items.headline: AegisAI, founded by former Google security execs, lands $36M to stop AI-driven spear phishing
items.summary: AegisAI co-founders developed AI agents that quickly analyze each message as a human would, paying attention to small anomalies that even the most elaborate checklist wouldn’t catch.

items.headline: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
items.summary: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of its workers, or around 93 employees, as reported earlier by 404 Media. In a memo to employees, Patreon CEO Jack Conte writes that the company isn't making these changes "because we believe AI replaces…

items.headline: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
items.summary: The Media Router is a tool that automatically selects the best image, video, or audio generation model for a request based on whether a developer prioritizes quality, speed or cost.

items.headline: OpenAI is making big claims as it rolls out ChatGPT Health to everyone
items.summary: OpenAI is rolling out ChatGPT Health to everyone in the US on Thursday, allowing more people to connect their medical records and health-tracking information to the chatbot. During a briefing, Ashley Alexander, OpenAI's vice president of…

items.headline: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all US users
items.summary: Users can also integrate their personal data from services like Apple Health, Function, and MyFitnessPal.

items.headline: Meta launched a new AI optimism ad set to a song about human extinction
items.summary: David Bowie's song "Five Years," which Meta used in a supposedly inspiring advertisement, is about humans learning that they have five years left to live before the apocalypse.

items.headline: Google just had its first negative cash flow quarter due to massive AI spending
items.summary: Google continues to report big quarterly revenue, but its AI spending has skyrocketed.

items.headline: Amazon cuts jobs from its artificial general intelligence team
items.summary: 1 points and 1 comments on Hacker News.

items.headline: Nvidia is sending GPUs to the moon
items.summary: If there's a place in the universe without GPUs, Nvidia is sending them there.

items.headline: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors
items.summary: Etched, founded by three Harvard dropouts, has created new chips and memory components that speed up inference on any AI model -- no GPUs required, it says.

items.headline: Google’s Gemini nears billion-user milestone
items.summary: Gemini had over 750 million monthly users in February.

items.headline: AI arms race in line for a reckoning after OpenAI hacking incident
items.summary: Aggressive training techniques sharpens threat of bad behavior by leading models.

items.headline: How much energy do data centers and artificial intelligence use?
items.summary: 69 points and 71 comments on Hacker News.

items.headline: Lawmakers prepare bill requiring AI ‘kill switch’
items.summary: Lawmakers are preparing to introduce an "AI Kill Switch Act" that would require AI companies to shut down or throttle their systems on orders from the Department of Homeland Security, according to a report from Politico. Reps. Ted Lieu…

items.headline: Free Copilot is turning into a demo as Microsoft shifts real features to paid
items.summary: 3 points and 0 comments on Hacker News.

items.headline: Apple’s OpenAI lawsuit is about who gets to define the post-smartphone era
items.summary: Today on Decoder, I’m talking with Hayden Field, The Verge’s senior AI reporter, about the major trade secrets lawsuit between Apple and OpenAI and what this tells us about OpenAI’s future. By now I’m sure most Decoder listeners are…

items.headline: AI Companies Are Trying to Hide a Staggering Amount of Debt
items.summary: 621 points and 310 comments on Hacker News.
